options.why: Multiplied $2\times5$ but dropped the numerator; you must add the $3$ to get $13$.
Multiplied the whole by the numerator ($2\times3$) instead of by the denominator $5$.
Wrote the digits $2$ and $3$ side by side instead of computing $2\times5+3=13$.
Multiplied $3\times4$ but forgot to add the numerator $1$.
Wrote the digits $3$ and $1$ together instead of computing $3\times4+1=13$.
Added $3+1$ over the denominator instead of $3\times4+1$.
Swapped the leftover and the denominator; the remainder $5$ goes over the original denominator $6$.
Rounded $23\div6$ up to $4$; the whole part is $3$ with remainder $5$.
Only took out two sixes ($12$), leaving $11$; another whole fits, giving $3\frac{5}{6}$.

questions.question_text: Write $\frac{12}{4}$ as a whole or mixed number.

options.why: $12\div4=3$ exactly with no remainder, so there is no fraction part.
$12\div4=3$ exactly; writing $2\frac{4}{4}$ leaves an extra whole uncounted.
Wrote the remainder $3$ after the point; you must keep dividing $7\div4=1.75$.
Reversed the division; it is $7\div4=1.75$, not $4\div7$.
Dropped the negative sign; the whole number is $-3$, so the result is negative.
Multiplied $3\times5$ then subtracted the numerator instead of adding: $15+2=17$.
Multiplied the denominator by the whole as well; keep the denominator as $5$.
